sections.title: SECTION 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best answer. Then on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 

Listen to an example.

On the recording, you hear:

(narrator)
(man) That exam was just awful. 
(woman) Oh, it could have been worse. 
(narrator) What does the woman mean? 

In your test book, you read:
(A) The exam was really awful. 
(B) It was the worst exam she had ever seen. 
(C) It couldn't have been more difficult. 
(D) It wasn't that hard. 

You learn from the conversation that the man thought the exam was very difficult and that the woman disagreed with the man. The best answer to the question, "What does the woman mean?" is (D), "It wasn't that hard." Therefore, the correct choice is (D).

questions.passage:
W: Are you enjoying your coffee?
M: It tastes extremely bitter this morning!
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The coffee is much better this morning.

reason.en: This option uses the word 'better,' which is a similar-sounding word trap for 'bitter,' violating Skill 3.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata 'better,' yang merupakan jebakan kata dengan bunyi serupa untuk 'bitter,' melanggar Skill 3.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets 'bitter' as 'good,' which is the opposite of the man's intended meaning.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru mengartikan 'bitter' sebagai 'good,' yang merupakan kebalikan dari maksud pria tersebut.

reason.en: This is the correct answer because it paraphrases the man's statement that the coffee is 'bitter' (unpleasant) as 'isn't very good' using synonyms.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar karena memparafrase pernyataan pria tersebut bahwa kopinya 'bitter' (tidak enak) menjadi 'isn't very good' menggunakan sinonim.

options.text: This morning he definitely wants some coffee.

reason.en: This option is irrelevant to the man's comment about the quality of the coffee he is currently drinking.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak relevan dengan komentar pria tersebut mengenai kualitas kopi yang sedang ia minum.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Can you tell me how often the philosophy class meets?
M: It meets twice a week, for an hour and a half each time.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The two classes meet in an hour and a half.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets the frequency and duration, suggesting the classes meet inside a specific time frame rather than describing the schedule.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menginterpretasikan frekuensi dan durasi, menyiratkan kelas bertemu dalam jangka waktu tertentu alih-alih menjelaskan jadwalnya.

options.text: The class meets three hours per week.

reason.en: The man states the class meets twice a week for an hour and a half each time. Mathematically, 2 times 1.5 hours equals 3 hours per week, which is a correct paraphrase.
reason.id: Pria tersebut menyatakan kelas bertemu dua kali seminggu selama satu setengah jam setiap pertemuan. Secara matematis, 2 kali 1,5 jam sama dengan 3 jam per minggu, yang merupakan parafrasa yang tepat.

options.text: Each half of the class is an hour long.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the duration of the class sessions, incorrectly dividing the time into halves.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menginterpretasikan durasi sesi kelas dengan membagi waktu menjadi dua bagian secara tidak tepat.

options.text: Two times a week the class meets for an hour.

reason.en: This option incorrectly states the duration of each meeting as one hour, contradicting the man's statement of an hour and a half.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menyatakan durasi setiap pertemuan sebagai satu jam, bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria tersebut yang menyebutkan satu setengah jam.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
I'm tired of just sitting here!
M: W: Relax. I'm sure that the flight will depart within a few minutes.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: A few minutes ago, the flight departed.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets the time frame, suggesting the flight has already left, which contradicts the woman's statement.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menginterpretasikan kerangka waktu, menyiratkan penerbangan sudah berangkat, yang bertentangan dengan pernyataan wanita tersebut.

options.text: The fight will start in a while.

reason.en: This option uses the word 'fight' instead of 'flight', which is a similar-sounding word trap (Skill 3) and does not match the context.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata 'fight' alih-alih 'flight', yang merupakan jebakan kata dengan bunyi serupa (Skill 3) dan tidak sesuai dengan konteks.

options.text: They are frightened about the departure.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the word 'flight' as 'frightened', which is a similar-sounding word trap (Skill 3) and is not supported by the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah mengartikan kata 'flight' sebagai 'frightened', yang merupakan jebakan kata dengan bunyi serupa (Skill 3) dan tidak didukung oleh dialog.

options.text: The plane is going to take off soon.

reason.en: This is the correct answer because it paraphrases 'the flight will depart within a few minutes' using the synonym 'take off soon'.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar karena memparafrasekan 'the flight will depart within a few minutes' menggunakan sinonim 'take off soon'.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: The science project is due next week.
M: I suppose I'll have to start working on it now.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He hasn't yet begun his project.

reason.en: The man says he will have to start working on it 'now', which implies he has not started yet. This is a correct paraphrase of the situation.
reason.id: Pria tersebut mengatakan dia harus mulai mengerjakannya 'sekarang', yang menyiratkan bahwa dia belum memulainya. Ini adalah parafrasa yang tepat dari situasi tersebut.

options.text: He's supposed to do his science project next week.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets the due date as the time he is supposed to do the work, rather than the deadline for completion.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menafsirkan tanggal jatuh tempo sebagai waktu dia seharusnya mengerjakan tugas, bukan tenggat waktu penyelesaian.

options.text: He needs to start working on changing the due date.

reason.en: The dialogue mentions the due date is next week, but the man does not express any intention to change that date.
reason.id: Dialog menyebutkan tanggal jatuh tempo adalah minggu depan, namun pria tersebut tidak menyatakan niat untuk mengubah tanggal tersebut.

options.text: He's been working steadily on his science project.

reason.en: This contradicts the man's statement that he has to start working on it now, implying he has not been working on it previously.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria tersebut bahwa dia harus mulai mengerjakannya sekarang, yang menyiratkan dia belum mengerjakannya sebelumnya.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line & Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
M: I'd like to order a dozen roses. Do you deliver?
W: Yes. We can deliver anywhere in the city by this afternoon.
questions.question: 5. WHERE DOES THIS CONVERSATION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

reason.en: The post office handles mail and packages, not fresh flowers.
reason.id: Kantor pos menangani surat dan paket, bukan bunga segar.

reason.en: The mention of 'a dozen roses' and 'deliver' strongly indicates a florist shop, which is the primary business for selling and delivering flowers.
reason.id: Penyebutan 'selusin mawar' dan 'mengirim' sangat mengindikasikan toko bunga, yang merupakan bisnis utama untuk menjual dan mengirim bunga.

reason.en: Restaurants serve food and drinks, not floral arrangements.
reason.id: Restoran menyajikan makanan dan minuman, bukan rangkaian bunga.

reason.en: A hospital delivery room is for childbirth, not for ordering floral deliveries.
reason.id: Ruang bersalin rumah sakit adalah untuk proses kelahiran, bukan untuk memesan pengiriman bunga.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 4: Draw Conclusions about Who, What, Where

questions.passage:
W: Did you enjoy the biology lecture?
M: The professor droned on and on about cell division.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The professor drowned the cells in a lab.

reason.en: This option is a trap using the word 'drowned', which sounds similar to 'droned' in the dialogue, but it is contextually incorrect.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah jebakan yang menggunakan kata 'drowned', yang terdengar mirip dengan 'droned' dalam dialog, namun secara konteks salah.

options.text: The lecture was long and boring.

reason.en: The phrase 'droned on and on' is an idiom meaning to speak in a dull, monotonous, and lengthy manner. This option correctly paraphrases the man's negative experience with the lecture.
reason.id: Frasa 'droned on and on' adalah idiom yang berarti berbicara dengan cara yang membosankan, monoton, dan panjang lebar. Opsi ini secara tepat memparafrasekan pengalaman negatif pria tersebut terhadap kuliah tersebut.

options.text: The professor divided the lecture into parts.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the phrase 'cell division' as a literal instruction for the professor to divide the lecture, which is not the intended meaning.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah mengartikan frasa 'cell division' sebagai instruksi harfiah bagi profesor untuk membagi kuliah, yang bukan merupakan makna yang dimaksud.

options.text: The biologist tried to sell the results of the experiment.

reason.en: This option uses words like 'biologist' and 'experiment' to create a false association with the topic of biology, but it does not reflect the man's opinion.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata-kata seperti 'biologist' dan 'experiment' untuk menciptakan asosiasi palsu dengan topik biologi, namun tidak mencerminkan pendapat pria tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 3: Avoid Similar Sounds

questions.passage:
M: What do I need to cash a check?
W: I have to see a driver's license and a credit card.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She needs to get a driver's license.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets the woman's statement as a personal need rather than a requirement for the man.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menafsirkan pernyataan wanita tersebut sebagai kebutuhan pribadi, bukan sebagai persyaratan bagi pria tersebut.

options.text: Two pieces of identification are necessary.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. It paraphrases the woman's requirement of a 'driver's license' and a 'credit card' as 'two pieces of identification'.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Opsi ini memparafrasekan persyaratan wanita tersebut mengenai 'SIM' dan 'kartu kredit' sebagai 'dua buah identitas'.

options.text: The man should check to see if he needs credit.

reason.en: This option uses the word 'check' from the dialogue but misinterprets the context, creating a distractor.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata 'check' dari dialog namun salah menafsirkan konteksnya, sehingga menjadi pengecoh.

options.text: A credit card can be used to get a driver's license.

reason.en: This option incorrectly links the credit card to obtaining a driver's license, which is not mentioned in the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menghubungkan kartu kredit dengan cara mendapatkan SIM, yang tidak disebutkan dalam dialog.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Have you been able to find an apartment yet?
M: It's difficult to find affordable housing in New York.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Housing within his budget is hard to locate.

reason.en: The phrase 'affordable housing' is paraphrased as 'housing within his budget', and 'difficult to find' is synonymous with 'hard to locate'. This aligns with Skill 2.
reason.id: Frasa 'affordable housing' diparafrasekan menjadi 'housing within his budget', dan 'difficult to find' bersinonim dengan 'hard to locate'. Ini sesuai dengan Skill 2.

options.text: It's hard to find his house in New York.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the word 'housing' as 'his house' and incorrectly suggests he is looking for a house he already owns, which is not mentioned.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah mengartikan kata 'housing' sebagai 'his house' dan secara keliru menyarankan bahwa dia sedang mencari rumah yang sudah dia miliki, yang tidak disebutkan.

options.text: He can't afford to move his house to New York.

reason.en: This option is a surface-level misinterpretation that confuses the context of finding an apartment with the act of moving an existing house.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah salah tafsir tingkat permukaan yang mencampuradukkan konteks mencari apartemen dengan tindakan memindahkan rumah yang sudah ada.

options.text: Housing in New York is unavailable.

reason.en: This is an extreme interpretation. The man says it is 'difficult' to find affordable housing, not that it is 'unavailable' (non-existent).
reason.id: Ini adalah interpretasi yang berlebihan. Pria tersebut mengatakan 'sulit' untuk menemukan perumahan yang terjangkau, bukan berarti 'tidak tersedia' (tidak ada).

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Why were you so late in getting home from work?
M: My boss had me finish all the month-end reports.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The boss was working on the reports.

reason.en: This option incorrectly identifies the boss as the one doing the work, whereas the man states the boss 'had' him finish the reports.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru mengidentifikasi bos sebagai orang yang mengerjakan laporan, padahal pria tersebut menyatakan bosnya 'menyuruh' dia menyelesaikan laporan.

options.text: He would have to finish the reports before the end of next month.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the time frame 'month-end' as 'next month', which is not supported by the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan kerangka waktu 'akhir bulan' menjadi 'bulan depan', yang tidak didukung oleh dialog.

options.text: He was directed to stay late and finish some work.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. The phrase 'had me finish' indicates a passive-causative structure where the man was directed or required by his boss to complete the work, explaining why he was late.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Frasa 'had me finish' menunjukkan struktur pasif-kausatif di mana pria tersebut diarahkan atau diminta oleh bosnya untuk menyelesaikan pekerjaan, yang menjelaskan mengapa dia terlambat.

options.text: He could finish the reports at home.

reason.en: This option contradicts the context, as the man was late specifically because he had to finish the work at the office, not at home.
reason.id: Opsi ini bertentangan dengan konteks, karena pria tersebut terlambat justru karena dia harus menyelesaikan pekerjaan di kantor, bukan di rumah.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 5: Listen for Who and What in Passives

questions.passage:
M: Ms. Jones did not look too happy as she left her classroom.
W: She was angered by her rowdy students.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The boisterous students made the teacher mad.

reason.en: This option correctly paraphrases the dialogue using synonyms: 'boisterous' for 'rowdy' and 'mad' for 'angered'. It accurately reflects that the students' behavior caused the teacher's emotional state.
reason.id: Opsi ini memparafrase dialog dengan tepat menggunakan sinonim: 'boisterous' untuk 'rowdy' dan 'mad' untuk 'angered'. Ini secara akurat mencerminkan bahwa perilaku siswa menyebabkan kondisi emosional guru tersebut.

options.text: The teacher angered the students with the exam results.

reason.en: This option incorrectly identifies the cause of the anger. The dialogue states the teacher was angered by the students, not that the teacher angered the students.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru mengidentifikasi penyebab kemarahan. Dialog menyatakan guru tersebut marah karena siswa, bukan guru yang membuat siswa marah.

options.text: The students were angry that the teacher was around.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the subject of the anger. The dialogue indicates the teacher was the one who was angered, not the students.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan subjek kemarahan. Dialog menunjukkan bahwa gurulah yang marah, bukan siswanya.

options.text: The angered students complained to the teacher.

reason.en: This option introduces information not present in the dialogue. There is no mention of a complaint being made by the students.
reason.id: Opsi ini memasukkan informasi yang tidak ada dalam dialog. Tidak ada penyebutan mengenai keluhan yang dibuat oleh siswa.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: The prices at this store are really outrageous!
M: You can say that again!
questions.question: 11. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

reason.en: This option contradicts the woman's statement that the prices are outrageous.
reason.id: Pilihan ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan wanita bahwa harga-harganya sangat mahal.

options.text: The store is too far out of town.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the word 'outrageous' as a reference to location rather than price.
reason.id: Pilihan ini salah mengartikan kata 'outrageous' sebagai referensi lokasi, bukan harga.

options.text: He would like the woman to repeat what she said.

reason.en: The phrase 'You can say that again' is an idiom for agreement, not a literal request for repetition.
reason.id: Frasa 'You can say that again' adalah idiom untuk menyatakan persetujuan, bukan permintaan literal untuk mengulang perkataan.

reason.en: The expression 'You can say that again' is a standard idiom used to show strong agreement with the previous speaker.
reason.id: Ekspresi 'You can say that again' adalah idiom standar yang digunakan untuk menunjukkan persetujuan kuat dengan pembicara sebelumnya.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
W: I don't like this weather very much.
M: We haven't see rain like this for many years!
questions.question: 12. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: It's rained unusually hard this year.

reason.en: The man uses a negative structure ('haven't seen rain like this') to emphasize that the current intensity of the rain is unusual compared to the past several years.
reason.id: Pria tersebut menggunakan struktur negatif ('haven't seen rain like this') untuk menekankan bahwa intensitas hujan saat ini tidak biasa dibandingkan dengan beberapa tahun terakhir.

options.text: There hasn't been any rain for many years.

reason.en: This is a trap that misinterprets the negative expression; the man is not saying there was no rain, but that the current intensity is unprecedented.
reason.id: Ini adalah jebakan yang salah menafsirkan ekspresi negatif; pria tersebut tidak mengatakan tidak ada hujan, melainkan intensitas saat ini belum pernah terjadi sebelumnya.

options.text: It's been many years since it rained.

reason.en: This is a surface-level interpretation that ignores the comparative context of the rain's intensity.
reason.id: Ini adalah interpretasi tingkat permukaan yang mengabaikan konteks perbandingan intensitas hujan.

reason.en: The man does not express his own opinion about the rain; he is only commenting on the rarity of the current weather conditions.
reason.id: Pria tersebut tidak mengungkapkan pendapat pribadinya tentang hujan; dia hanya mengomentari kelangkaan kondisi cuaca saat ini.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
M: Professor Martin, what do you think of the composition that I turned in last week?
W: Without question, you need to improve the quality of your writing.
questions.question: 13. WHAT DOES PROFESSOR MARTIN SAY ABOUT THE STUDENT?

options.text: He needs to do a better job writing questions.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it misinterprets the context of 'writing' as 'writing questions' instead of the student's composition.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menyalahartikan konteks 'writing' sebagai 'menulis pertanyaan' alih-alih komposisi tulisan siswa tersebut.

options.text: He certainly must make his writing better.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. It uses synonyms to paraphrase the professor's statement: 'improve the quality of your writing' is synonymous with 'make his writing better'.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Opsi ini menggunakan sinonim untuk memparafrase pernyataan profesor: 'improve the quality of your writing' bersinonim dengan 'make his writing better'.

options.text: Without the questions, he cannot write the answers.

reason.en: This option is incorrect as it introduces irrelevant information about 'questions' and 'answers' that was not mentioned in the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena memasukkan informasi yang tidak relevan mengenai 'pertanyaan' dan 'jawaban' yang tidak disebutkan dalam dialog.

options.text: He needs to understand the written questions better.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it focuses on the student's ability to understand questions, whereas the professor specifically addressed the quality of the student's written composition.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena berfokus pada kemampuan siswa memahami pertanyaan, padahal profesor secara spesifik membahas kualitas komposisi tulisan siswa tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Where should I go next?
M: You must stand in this line so that the agent can check your passport.
(D: I He can check her passport instead of the agent.
questions.question: 14. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The agent was standing in line with his passport.

reason.en: This option incorrectly identifies the agent as the person standing in line, which contradicts the man's instruction to the woman.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru mengidentifikasi agen sebagai orang yang mengantre, yang bertentangan dengan instruksi pria tersebut kepada wanita itu.

options.text: The line to get new passports is very long.

reason.en: This option focuses on the length of the line, which is not mentioned in the dialogue; it is a distractor.
reason.id: Opsi ini berfokus pada panjang antrean, yang tidak disebutkan dalam percakapan; ini adalah pengecoh.

options.text: The woman must wait her turn to get her passport checked.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. It paraphrases the man's instruction to 'stand in this line' so the agent can 'check your passport' as waiting for her turn.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Opsi ini memparafrase instruksi pria tersebut untuk 'berdiri di antrean ini' agar agen dapat 'memeriksa paspor Anda' sebagai menunggu giliran.

options.text: He can check her passport instead of the agent.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it suggests the man will perform the check, whereas the dialogue states the agent must do it.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menyiratkan pria tersebut yang akan melakukan pemeriksaan, padahal percakapan menyatakan agen yang harus melakukannya.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: Did Paul get his work done?
W: He couldn't finish the assignment because the library was closed.
questions.question: 15. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT PAUL?

options.text: He couldn't finish closing the library book.

reason.en: This option uses similar-sounding words like 'closing' and 'library' to create a trap, which is incorrect according to Skill 3.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata-kata yang terdengar mirip seperti 'closing' dan 'library' untuk membuat jebakan, yang tidak tepat menurut Skill 3.

options.text: He hadn't finished the library assignment, but he was close.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the meaning of the dialogue by suggesting he was 'close' to finishing, which is not stated.
reason.id: Opsi ini menyalahartikan makna dialog dengan menyarankan bahwa dia 'hampir' selesai, yang tidak disebutkan dalam teks.

options.text: He was working on the assignment when the library closed.

reason.en: This option incorrectly implies he was in the middle of working when it closed, whereas the dialogue states he could not finish because it was closed.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menyiratkan bahwa dia sedang bekerja saat perpustakaan tutup, padahal dialog menyatakan dia tidak bisa menyelesaikannya karena perpustakaan tutup.

options.text: i His homework was incomplete because the library wasn't open.

reason.en: This is the correct answer as it uses synonyms: 'homework' for 'assignment' and 'wasn't open' for 'was closed', accurately reflecting the woman's statement.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar karena menggunakan sinonim: 'homework' untuk 'assignment' dan 'wasn't open' untuk 'was closed', yang secara akurat mencerminkan pernyataan wanita tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: The lawyer spent hours and hours working on that case.
M: It's true that he prepared hard for the case, but his work was for nothing.
questions.question: 16. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: All the lawyer's preparation did no good.

reason.en: The idiom 'for nothing' means without success or in vain. The man confirms the lawyer worked hard but achieved no result, which matches this option.
reason.id: Idiom 'for nothing' berarti tanpa hasil atau sia-sia. Pria tersebut mengonfirmasi bahwa pengacara bekerja keras namun tidak membuahkan hasil, yang sesuai dengan pilihan ini.

options.text: The lawyer prepared nothing for the case.

reason.en: This contradicts the dialogue, which explicitly states that the lawyer 'prepared hard' for the case.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan dialog, yang secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa pengacara tersebut 'mempersiapkan dengan keras' untuk kasus itu.

groups.passage:
N: Listen to a conversation on a university campus. 

M: You seem to know your way around campus. Have you been here long? 
w: I'm a senior literature major. I'll be graduating next June. 
M: Your major is literature? Mine is, too.But I'm just beginning my work in my major. I just transferred to this university from a junior college. Perhaps you could tell me about the courses you've got to take for a literature major. 
w: Well, for a literature major you need to take eight courses, three required courses and five electives. First, you have to take "Survey of World Literature, Parts One and Two." This is really two courses, and it'll take two semesters, and it's required for all literature majors. The other course required for all literature majors is "Introduction to Literary Analysis." 
M: You mean, if I want to specialize in American literature, I still must take two semesters of World literature?  
w: Yes, because the two semesters are required for all literature majors. 
M: But I only want to study American literature! 
w: At least you can take all of your five elective courses in the area that you want. 
M: That's what I'll do, then.
groups.label: Q 31-34

groups.passage:
Listen to a conversation between two friends. 
w: Wasn't that a fascinating lecture on dolphins? I didn't know that dolphins traveled in such large groups, or were able to communicate with other members of their group with those whistlelike sounds. 
M: And they also use clicks as a sort of sonar. 
w: I really couldn't understand that part of the lecture. You could? 
M: Yes, the dolphins use clicks to identify objects in the water; they can even identify tiny objects more than 100 meters away using these clicks. Scientists believe that a dolphin may even have a sonar-like image in its brain of a distant object so that it can identify the object long before the dolphin can actually see the object. 
w: So the dolphins use these clicks mostly to identify objects in the water? 
M: I think so, and they have considerably more ability to do this than humans do. 
w: It is hard to believe that, in addition to these sonar clicks, dolphins are actually learning some human language. 
M: Yes, I believe that the lecturer said that some dolphins had already learned around fifty human commands, and that those dolphins were able to understand not only individual words but words clustered together in sentences! 
w: Dolphins must certainly be amazing animals to do all of that. 
M:  I'm sure they are, and we're only just beginning to find out how intelligent they are.

The gymnasium also includes an up-to-date exercise room with a large variety of the latest equipment; the exercise room is open to any student with valid student I.D., not just members of athletic teams. The pool complex is behind the gymnasium, and that is also open for general student use, except when the 
swim team, the diving team, or the water polo team is practicing. 

To the left, you can see the tennis courts and outdoor volleyball courts. It is possible to take instruction classes in these sports, or you are welcome to sign up for court time at the Athletic Department office if you just want to play with some of your friends. 

These are just some of the sports facilities that are available to you here, but I think you can see that this university makes an effort to provide the best opportunity for its students to take part in sports. Now, let's continue on to the Art Center.

groups.passage:
(narrator) Listen to a talk given by a professor. 
(woman) Today's lecture is on the difference between the two literary styles of realism and naturalism. These two styles have in common a faithfulness to actual experience and a mistrust of idealism. 

Although they do have several similarities, realism and naturalism should be clearly differentiated. The realist objectively reports on events, with the accuracy of the description as the prime motive. The naturalist, on the other hand, has more of a philosophic bent; naturalist writings express the writer's philosophy that human actions are determined by natural laws such as heredity and environment.

In this section of the test, you will have an opportunity to demonstrate your ability to understand conversations and talks in English. There are three parts to this section. Answer all the questions on the basis of what is stated or implied by the speakers you hear. Do not take notes or write in your test book at any time. Do not turn the pages until you are told to do so.

sections.title: STRUCTURE AND WRITTEN EXPRESSION

parts.title: Structure
parts.instruction:
This section is designed to measure your ability to recognize language that is appropriate for standard written English. There are two types of questions in this section, with special directions for each type.

Questions 1-15 are incomplete sentences. Beneath each sentence you will see four words or phrases, marked (A), (B), (C), and (D). Choose the one word or phrase that best completes the sentence. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. Fill in the space so that the letter inside the oval cannot be seen. 

Look at the following examples 
The president ____________ the election by a landslide.
(A) won 
(B) he won 
(C) yesterday 
(D) fortunately 

The sentence should read, "The president won the election by a landslide." Therefore, you should choose (A).

questions.question: The North Pole ___________ a latitude of 90 degrees north.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence because 'it' acts as a redundant subject, and there is no connector to join two clauses.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang rancu karena 'it' bertindak sebagai subjek redundan, dan tidak ada kata penghubung untuk menggabungkan dua klausa.

reason.en: The verb 'is having' is incorrect because 'have' in the sense of possession or state is a stative verb and is not typically used in the continuous form.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'is having' salah karena 'have' dalam konteks kepemilikan atau keadaan adalah stative verb dan biasanya tidak digunakan dalam bentuk continuous.

reason.en: This option creates a sentence fragment. 'Which' is a relative pronoun that requires an independent clause to follow, but here it leaves the sentence without a main verb.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan fragmen kalimat. 'Which' adalah kata ganti relatif yang memerlukan klausa independen setelahnya, namun di sini kalimat tersebut menjadi tidak memiliki kata kerja utama.

reason.en: This is the correct option. The sentence requires a main verb to complete the subject 'The North Pole'. 'Has' functions as the simple present tense verb.
reason.id: Ini adalah opsi yang benar. Kalimat tersebut memerlukan kata kerja utama untuk melengkapi subjek 'The North Pole'. 'Has' berfungsi sebagai kata kerja bentuk simple present.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: The city of Beverly Hills is surrounded on ___________ the city of Los Angeles.

reason.en: This option creates a fragment. 'Surrounded on its sides the city' lacks the necessary preposition 'by' to connect the agent of the passive verb.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan fragmen. 'Surrounded on its sides the city' tidak memiliki kata depan 'by' yang diperlukan untuk menghubungkan pelaku dengan kata kerja pasif.

reason.en: This option is grammatically incorrect because it introduces a clause ('the sides are') where a prepositional phrase is required to complete the passive construction.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah secara tata bahasa karena memasukkan klausa ('the sides are') di mana frasa preposisional diperlukan untuk melengkapi konstruksi pasif.

reason.en: This option is structurally incoherent. It adds an unnecessary subject and verb ('it is') that disrupt the flow of the sentence.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak koheren secara struktur. Opsi ini menambahkan subjek dan kata kerja yang tidak perlu ('it is') yang mengganggu alur kalimat.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. It completes the passive voice construction 'is surrounded on... by...' by providing the object of the preposition 'by' (the city of Los Angeles).
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Opsi ini melengkapi konstruksi kalimat pasif 'is surrounded on... by...' dengan menyediakan objek dari kata depan 'by' (the city of Los Angeles).

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 2: Be Careful of Objects of Prepositions

questions.question: ___________ greyhound, can achieve speeds up to thirty-six miles per hour.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it lacks a noun to serve as the subject, and the sentence would lack an appositive structure to describe the subject.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena tidak memiliki kata benda untuk berfungsi sebagai subjek, dan kalimat tersebut akan kekurangan struktur aposisi untuk mendeskripsikan subjek.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it provides an adjective phrase but fails to include the noun that the appositive phrase 'greyhound' would modify.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menyediakan frasa kata sifat tetapi gagal menyertakan kata benda yang akan dimodifikasi oleh frasa aposisi 'greyhound'.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it creates a noun phrase, but it fails to provide the necessary appositive structure (the comma and the noun) to correctly link 'The fastest dog' with 'greyhound'.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena membentuk frasa kata benda, tetapi gagal menyediakan struktur aposisi yang diperlukan (koma dan kata benda) untuk menghubungkan 'The fastest dog' dengan 'greyhound' secara tepat.

reason.en: [please evaluate-manual] Based on the structure of the sentence, 'The fastest dog, the greyhound,' functions as the subject followed by an appositive. However, the original sentence structure 'The fastest dog, the greyhound, can achieve...' is grammatically sound as an appositive construction.
reason.id: [please evaluate-manual] Berdasarkan struktur kalimat, 'The fastest dog, the greyhound,' berfungsi sebagai subjek yang diikuti oleh aposisi. Struktur kalimat 'The fastest dog, the greyhound, can achieve...' adalah konstruksi aposisi yang benar secara gramatikal.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 3: Be Careful of Appositives

questions.question: Marmots spend their time foraging among meadow plants and flowers or__________ on rocky cliffs.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'gets' is a singular verb form that does not maintain parallelism with the gerund 'foraging'.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'gets' adalah bentuk kata kerja tunggal yang tidak menjaga kesejajaran (paralelisme) dengan gerund 'foraging'.

reason.en: This option is correct because it uses the gerund 'sunning', which is parallel to the gerund 'foraging' connected by the conjunction 'or'.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar karena menggunakan gerund 'sunning', yang sejajar dengan gerund 'foraging' yang dihubungkan oleh kata hubung 'or'.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'the sun' is a noun phrase, which does not match the grammatical form of the gerund 'foraging'.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'the sun' adalah frasa kata benda, yang tidak sesuai dengan bentuk gramatikal gerund 'foraging'.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'sunny' is an adjective, which cannot function as a parallel element to the gerund 'foraging'.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'sunny' adalah kata sifat, yang tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai elemen yang sejajar dengan gerund 'foraging'.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 24: Parallel Structure

questions.question: The greenhouse effect occurs _______________ heat radiated from the Sun.

options.text: when does the Earth s atmosphere trap

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it uses interrogative word order ('does the Earth's atmosphere trap') inside a dependent clause, which is grammatically invalid.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menggunakan urutan kata tanya ('does the Earth's atmosphere trap') di dalam klausa dependen, yang secara tata bahasa tidak valid.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it lacks a connector to join the two clauses, resulting in a run-on sentence structure.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena tidak memiliki kata penghubung untuk menyambungkan dua klausa, sehingga menghasilkan struktur kalimat yang tidak beraturan (run-on).

reason.en: This option is correct. 'When' acts as an adverb connector of time/condition, and 'the Earth's atmosphere traps' provides the necessary subject and verb for the dependent clause.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar. 'When' berfungsi sebagai kata penghubung keterangan waktu/kondisi, dan 'the Earth's atmosphere traps' menyediakan subjek serta kata kerja yang diperlukan untuk klausa dependen.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it lacks a connector to link the main clause with the dependent clause, making the sentence grammatically incomplete.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena tidak memiliki kata penghubung untuk menghubungkan klausa utama dengan klausa dependen, sehingga kalimat menjadi tidak lengkap secara tata bahasa.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 7: Use Adverb Time and Cause Connectors Correctly

questions.question: The Rose Bowl, ________ place on New Year's Day, is the oldest postseason collegiate football game in the United States.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence because it adds a verb without a connector, resulting in two main verbs ('takes' and 'is') for one subject ('The Rose Bowl') without a conjunction.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat yang tidak gramatikal (run-on) karena menambahkan kata kerja tanpa konektor, sehingga terdapat dua kata kerja utama ('takes' dan 'is') untuk satu subjek ('The Rose Bowl') tanpa kata hubung.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because adding 'it' creates a redundant subject. The sentence already has 'The Rose Bowl' as the subject, so 'it' is unnecessary and grammatically incorrect.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena penambahan 'it' menciptakan subjek yang redundan. Kalimat tersebut sudah memiliki 'The Rose Bowl' sebagai subjek, sehingga 'it' tidak diperlukan dan secara tata bahasa salah.

reason.en: This is the correct option. 'Which' acts as an adjective clause connector and subject, correctly introducing the clause 'which takes place on New Year's Day' to modify the noun 'The Rose Bowl'.
reason.id: Ini adalah opsi yang benar. 'Which' berfungsi sebagai konektor klausa adjektiva sekaligus subjek, yang secara tepat memperkenalkan klausa 'which takes place on New Year's Day' untuk menerangkan kata benda 'The Rose Bowl'.

reason.en: This option uses the past tense 'took' without a connector, which fails to create a proper relative clause and leaves the sentence with two main verbs ('took' and 'is') incorrectly joined.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan bentuk lampau 'took' tanpa konektor, yang gagal membentuk klausa relatif yang tepat dan menyebabkan kalimat memiliki dua kata kerja utama ('took' dan 'is') yang digabungkan secara tidak benar.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 11: Use Adjective Clause Connectors Correctly

questions.question: Experiments ________ represent a giant step into the medicine of the future.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. 'Using gene therapy' is a reduced adjective clause (originally 'that use gene therapy') that modifies the subject 'Experiments'. The main verb of the sentence is 'represent'.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. 'Using gene therapy' adalah klausa adjektiva yang diperpendek (awalnya 'that use gene therapy') yang memodifikasi subjek 'Experiments'. Kata kerja utama kalimat ini adalah 'represent'.

reason.en: This is incorrect because it creates a sentence with two main verbs ('use' and 'represent') without a proper connector, leading to a run-on structure.
reason.id: Ini salah karena menciptakan kalimat dengan dua kata kerja utama ('use' dan 'represent') tanpa konektor yang tepat, sehingga menyebabkan struktur kalimat yang tidak baku.

reason.en: This is incorrect because 'they use' creates a second clause without a connector. A sentence cannot have two subjects and two verbs joined simply by placing them next to each other.
reason.id: Ini salah karena 'they use' menciptakan klausa kedua tanpa konektor. Sebuah kalimat tidak bisa memiliki dua subjek dan dua kata kerja yang digabungkan hanya dengan meletakkannya bersebelahan.

reason.en: This is incorrect because it creates a noun phrase that does not function as a modifier for 'Experiments', and it lacks the necessary structure to connect the two clauses.
reason.id: Ini salah karena menciptakan frasa kata benda yang tidak berfungsi sebagai penjelas untuk 'Experiments', dan tidak memiliki struktur yang diperlukan untuk menghubungkan kedua klausa tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 4: Be Careful of Present Participles

questions.question: ______________ off the Hawaiian coastline are living, others are dead.

options.text: While some types of coral reefs

reason.en: This option is correct because it uses the adverb connector 'While' to create a dependent clause ('While some types of coral reefs [are] off the Hawaiian coastline'), which is then followed by an independent clause ('others are dead'). This structure correctly joins two clauses with a comma.
reason.id: Opsi ini benar karena menggunakan konektor adverbia 'While' untuk membentuk klausa dependen ('While some types of coral reefs [are] off the Hawaiian coastline'), yang kemudian diikuti oleh klausa independen ('others are dead'). Struktur ini menghubungkan dua klausa dengan benar menggunakan koma.

reason.en: This option creates a run-on sentence. If you insert 'Some types of coral reefs', the sentence becomes 'Some types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living, others are dead.' This results in two independent clauses joined only by a comma, which is grammatically incorrect without a conjunction.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat run-on. Jika Anda memasukkan 'Some types of coral reefs', kalimatnya menjadi 'Some types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living, others are dead.' Ini menghasilkan dua klausa independen yang hanya dihubungkan oleh koma, yang secara tata bahasa salah tanpa kata hubung.

options.text: There are many types of coral reefs

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it creates a redundant structure. 'There are many types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living...' results in two verbs ('are' and 'are') fighting for the same subject, making the sentence structurally incoherent.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menciptakan struktur yang redundan. 'There are many types of coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living...' menghasilkan dua kata kerja ('are' dan 'are') yang memperebutkan subjek yang sama, membuat kalimat tersebut tidak koheren secara struktural.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it leaves the sentence as a fragment. 'Coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living, others are dead' would require a conjunction or a semicolon to properly connect the two independent clauses.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menyisakan kalimat sebagai fragmen. 'Coral reefs off the Hawaiian coastline are living, others are dead' akan memerlukan kata hubung atau titik koma untuk menghubungkan dua klausa independen tersebut dengan benar.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 8: Use Other Adverb Connectors Correctly

questions.question: Nimbostratus clouds are thick, dark gray clouds ____________ forebode rain.

reason.en: The word 'what' cannot be used as an adjective clause connector to modify a noun like 'clouds'.
reason.id: Kata 'what' tidak dapat digunakan sebagai konektor klausa adjektiva untuk menerangkan kata benda seperti 'clouds'.

reason.en: The relative pronoun 'which' is the correct connector to introduce an adjective clause modifying the noun 'clouds', acting as the subject of the verb 'forebode'.
reason.id: Kata ganti relatif 'which' adalah konektor yang tepat untuk memperkenalkan klausa adjektiva yang menerangkan kata benda 'clouds', berfungsi sebagai subjek dari kata kerja 'forebode'.

reason.en: Adding 'they' creates a redundant subject; 'what' is also grammatically incorrect here as an adjective clause connector.
reason.id: Menambahkan 'they' menciptakan subjek yang redundan; 'what' juga secara tata bahasa salah di sini sebagai konektor klausa adjektiva.

reason.en: Using 'which they' is incorrect because 'which' already functions as the subject of the verb 'forebode'. Adding 'they' makes the clause ungrammatical.
reason.id: Menggunakan 'which they' salah karena 'which' sudah berfungsi sebagai subjek dari kata kerja 'forebode'. Menambahkan 'they' membuat klausa tersebut tidak gramatikal.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 11: Use Adjective Clause Connectors Correctly

questions.question: Some economists now suggest that home equity loans are merely a new trap to push consumers beyond ______

reason.en: This option is grammatically incomplete because it lacks a noun clause connector to function as the object of the preposition 'beyond'.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak lengkap secara gramatikal karena kekurangan konektor klausa kata benda untuk berfungsi sebagai objek dari preposisi 'beyond'.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'it' is redundant; the noun clause 'what they can afford' would be correct, but 'they can afford it' creates a double object structure.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'it' bersifat redundan; klausa kata benda 'what they can afford' akan benar, namun 'they can afford it' menciptakan struktur objek ganda.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. 'What is affordable' acts as a noun clause functioning as the object of the preposition 'beyond'.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. 'What is affordable' bertindak sebagai klausa kata benda yang berfungsi sebagai objek dari preposisi 'beyond'.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'able to afford' is an adjective phrase and cannot function as the object of a preposition in this context.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'able to afford' adalah frasa kata sifat dan tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai objek preposisi dalam konteks ini.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 9: Use Noun Clause Connectors Correctly

questions.question: People who reverse the letters of words __________ to read suffer from dyslexia.

reason.en: This is the correct reduced adverbial clause. 'When trying to read' is a reduction of 'when they are trying to read', which maintains the active voice and correct subject-verb agreement with the main subject 'People'.
reason.id: Ini adalah klausa adverbial yang direduksi dengan benar. 'When trying to read' adalah bentuk ringkas dari 'when they are trying to read', yang mempertahankan bentuk aktif dan kesesuaian subjek-predikat dengan subjek utama 'People'.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it creates a tense mismatch. 'If they tried' uses the past tense, which does not fit the general present-tense context of the sentence describing a condition for people with dyslexia.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menciptakan ketidaksesuaian tenses. 'If they tried' menggunakan bentuk lampau, yang tidak sesuai dengan konteks kalimat yang menggunakan present tense untuk mendeskripsikan kondisi umum penderita disleksia.

reason.en: This option is grammatically incorrect. 'When tried' implies a passive meaning ('when they are tried'), which is illogical in this context because people are not being 'tried' (tested); they are the ones performing the action of reading.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah secara tata bahasa. 'When tried' menyiratkan makna pasif ('ketika mereka diuji'), yang tidak logis dalam konteks ini karena orang tersebut tidak sedang 'diuji', melainkan mereka sendiri yang melakukan tindakan membaca.

reason.en: This option is incorrect due to a pronoun-antecedent agreement error. The subject 'People' is plural, so the singular pronoun 'he' and the singular verb 'tries' cannot be used to refer back to it.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena kesalahan kesesuaian kata ganti. Subjek 'People' berbentuk jamak, sehingga kata ganti tunggal 'he' dan kata kerja tunggal 'tries' tidak dapat digunakan untuk merujuk kembali ke subjek tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 13: Reduced Clauses

questions.question: Featured at the Henry Ford Museum _______ of antique cars dating from 1865.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. The sentence begins with a prepositional phrase 'Featured at the Henry Ford Museum', which triggers subject-verb inversion. 'An exhibit' is the subject and 'is' is the verb, correctly placed after the subject.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Kalimat dimulai dengan frasa preposisi 'Featured at the Henry Ford Museum', yang memicu inversi subjek-predikat. 'An exhibit' adalah subjek dan 'is' adalah kata kerjanya, yang ditempatkan dengan benar setelah subjek.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it lacks a main verb. The sentence would be a fragment without a verb to complete the clause.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena tidak memiliki kata kerja utama. Kalimat tersebut akan menjadi fragmen tanpa kata kerja untuk melengkapi klausa.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it creates a double subject. 'An exhibit' would be the subject, but placing 'is' after it in this specific inverted structure creates a redundant or grammatically awkward construction.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menciptakan subjek ganda. 'An exhibit' akan menjadi subjek, namun menempatkan 'is' setelahnya dalam struktur inversi khusus ini menciptakan konstruksi yang redundan atau canggung secara tata bahasa.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'which is an exhibit' creates a dependent clause that lacks a main verb for the entire sentence, leaving the sentence incomplete.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'which is an exhibit' menciptakan klausa dependen yang tidak memiliki kata kerja utama untuk keseluruhan kalimat, sehingga kalimat menjadi tidak lengkap.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 15: Subject/Verb Inversion

questions.question: Rubber _________ from vulcanized silicones with a high molecular weight is difficult to distinguish from natural rubber.

reason.en: This option creates a second verb ('is produced') without a connector, leading to a run-on sentence structure.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kata kerja kedua ('is produced') tanpa kata penghubung, yang menyebabkan struktur kalimat menjadi tidak tepat (run-on).

reason.en: A present participle ('producing') used as an adjective would imply the rubber is actively performing the action of producing, which is logically incorrect.
reason.id: Present participle ('producing') yang digunakan sebagai kata sifat akan menyiratkan bahwa karet tersebut secara aktif melakukan tindakan memproduksi, yang secara logika tidak tepat.

reason.en: This creates a relative clause, but 'that produces' implies the rubber is the agent performing the production, which is logically incorrect.
reason.id: Ini menciptakan klausa relatif, namun 'that produces' menyiratkan bahwa karet tersebut adalah agen yang melakukan produksi, yang secara logika tidak tepat.

reason.en: This is the correct reduced relative clause. 'Produced' acts as a past participle adjective modifying 'Rubber', meaning 'Rubber (which is) produced from...'.
reason.id: Ini adalah klausa relatif yang diperpendek (reduced relative clause) yang benar. 'Produced' berfungsi sebagai kata sifat past participle yang menerangkan 'Rubber', yang berarti 'Karet (yang) diproduksi dari...'.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 5: Be Careful of Past Participles

questions.question: _________ appears considerably larger at the horizon than it does overhead is merely an optical illusion.

reason.en: This option creates a sentence with two subjects ('The Moon' and 'it') and two verbs ('appears' and 'is') without a proper connector, leading to a run-on structure.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan kalimat dengan dua subjek ('The Moon' dan 'it') dan dua kata kerja ('appears' dan 'is') tanpa konektor yang tepat, sehingga strukturnya menjadi tidak gramatikal.

reason.en: This is correct because 'That the Moon appears considerably larger at the horizon than it does overhead' functions as a noun clause acting as the subject of the main verb 'is'.
reason.id: Ini benar karena 'That the Moon appears considerably larger at the horizon than it does overhead' berfungsi sebagai klausa kata benda (noun clause) yang menjadi subjek dari kata kerja utama 'is'.

reason.en: This option creates an adverbial clause ('When the Moon appears...'), but the sentence would then lack a main subject for the verb 'is'.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan klausa keterangan ('When the Moon appears...'), namun kalimat tersebut kemudian akan kekurangan subjek utama untuk kata kerja 'is'.

reason.en: This option creates an adjective clause ('The Moon which appears...'), but the sentence would then lack a main verb for the subject 'The Moon'.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan klausa kata sifat ('The Moon which appears...'), namun kalimat tersebut kemudian akan kekurangan kata kerja utama untuk subjek 'The Moon'.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 9: Use Noun Clause Connectors Correctly

questions.question: According to the World Health Organization, ________ any of the six most dangerous diseases to break out, it could be cause for quarantine.

reason.en: This is a conditional sentence with an inverted structure. The full form is 'if there were any of the six...'. When 'if' is omitted, the auxiliary verb 'were' moves to the front of the subject 'there'.
reason.id: Ini adalah kalimat pengandaian (conditional) dengan struktur inversi. Bentuk lengkapnya adalah 'if there were any of the six...'. Ketika 'if' dihilangkan, kata kerja bantu 'were' berpindah ke depan subjek 'there'.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it creates a double subject ('they' and 'any of the six...'). Furthermore, it lacks the necessary inversion required for an 'if'-less conditional clause.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menciptakan subjek ganda ('they' dan 'any of the six...'). Selain itu, opsi ini tidak memiliki inversi yang diperlukan untuk klausa pengandaian tanpa 'if'.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because it lacks the inversion required when the 'if' is omitted. 'There were' would only be correct if the word 'if' were present in the sentence.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena tidak memiliki inversi yang diperlukan saat 'if' dihilangkan. 'There were' hanya akan benar jika kata 'if' ada di dalam kalimat.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'were they' implies a question structure or a different subject-verb inversion that does not fit the context of a conditional clause starting with 'any of the six...'.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'were they' menyiratkan struktur pertanyaan atau inversi subjek-kata kerja yang tidak sesuai dengan konteks klausa pengandaian yang dimulai dengan 'any of the six...'.

questions.tested_skill: Structure - Skill 15: Subject/Verb Inversion

parts.title: Written Expression
parts.instruction:
In questions 16-40, each sentence has four underlined words or phrases. The four 
underlined parts of the sentence are marked (A), (B), (C), and (D). Identify the one underlined word 
or phrase that must be changed in order for the sentence to be correct. Then, on your answer sheet, 
find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you 
have chosen.

questions.question: On ~[the floor of]~(A) the Pacific Ocean ~[is]~(B) hundreds of ~[flat-topped]~(C) mountains ~[more than a]~(D) mile beneath sea level.

reason.en: This is a prepositional phrase acting as an introductory adverbial of place. It is grammatically correct.
reason.id: Ini adalah frasa preposisional yang berfungsi sebagai keterangan tempat di awal kalimat. Penggunaannya sudah benar secara tata bahasa.

reason.en: The verb 'is' is incorrect because it must agree with the plural subject 'hundreds of flat-topped mountains'. Since the sentence uses subject-verb inversion due to the introductory prepositional phrase, the verb should be 'are'.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'is' salah karena harus sesuai dengan subjek jamak 'hundreds of flat-topped mountains'. Karena kalimat ini menggunakan inversi subjek-kata kerja akibat frasa preposisional di awal, kata kerja yang tepat adalah 'are'.

reason.en: This is a compound adjective correctly modifying the noun 'mountains'.
reason.id: Ini adalah kata sifat majemuk yang memodifikasi kata benda 'mountains' dengan tepat.

reason.en: This phrase correctly uses the comparative structure 'more than' followed by a singular noun phrase 'a mile'.
reason.id: Frasa ini menggunakan struktur perbandingan 'more than' dengan benar, diikuti oleh frasa kata benda tunggal 'a mile'.

questions.tested_skill: Structure & Written Expression - Skill 15: Subject/Verb Inversion

questions.question: Because of the flourish with ~[which]~(A) John Hancock signed the Declaration of Independence, ~[his]~(B) name ~[become]~(C) ~[synonymous]~(D) with signature.

reason.en: The relative pronoun 'which' is used correctly here to refer to the noun 'flourish', functioning as the object of the preposition 'with'.
reason.id: Kata ganti relatif 'which' digunakan dengan benar di sini untuk merujuk pada kata benda 'flourish', berfungsi sebagai objek dari preposisi 'with'.

reason.en: The possessive adjective 'his' is used correctly to modify the noun 'name', indicating that the name belongs to John Hancock.
reason.id: Kata sifat kepemilikan 'his' digunakan dengan benar untuk memodifikasi kata benda 'name', menunjukkan bahwa nama tersebut milik John Hancock.

reason.en: The verb 'become' is incorrect because the sentence describes a past event (the signing of the Declaration of Independence). It should be in the simple past tense, 'became'.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'become' salah karena kalimat tersebut mendeskripsikan peristiwa masa lalu (penandatanganan Deklarasi Kemerdekaan). Seharusnya menggunakan bentuk lampau sederhana, yaitu 'became'.

reason.en: The adjective 'synonymous' is used correctly after the linking verb to describe the subject 'his name', and it is correctly paired with the preposition 'with'.
reason.id: Kata sifat 'synonymous' digunakan dengan benar setelah kata kerja penghubung untuk mendeskripsikan subjek 'his name', dan dipasangkan dengan benar dengan preposisi 'with'.

questions.tested_skill: Structure and Written Expression - Skill 33: Use of the Verb (Tense)

questions.question: Segregation in ~[public]~(A) schools ~[was declare]~(B) ~[unconstitutional]~(C) by the Supreme Court ~[in 1954]~(D).

reason.en: The word 'public' is an adjective correctly modifying the noun 'schools'.
reason.id: Kata 'public' adalah kata sifat yang secara tepat menerangkan kata benda 'schools'.

reason.en: This is the error. The passive voice requires the form 'be + past participle'. Since the subject 'Segregation' is singular and the action happened in the past, it should be 'was declared'.
reason.id: Ini adalah bagian yang salah. Kalimat pasif memerlukan bentuk 'be + past participle'. Karena subjek 'Segregation' tunggal dan tindakan terjadi di masa lalu, seharusnya menjadi 'was declared'.

reason.en: The word 'unconstitutional' is an adjective functioning as a subject complement, which is grammatically correct after the passive verb.
reason.id: Kata 'unconstitutional' adalah kata sifat yang berfungsi sebagai pelengkap subjek, yang secara tata bahasa benar setelah kata kerja pasif.

reason.en: The phrase 'in 1954' is a correct prepositional phrase indicating the time of the action.
reason.id: Frasa 'in 1954' adalah frasa preposisional yang benar untuk menunjukkan waktu terjadinya tindakan.

questions.tested_skill: Structure & Written Expression - Skill 37: Passive Verbs

questions.question: Sirius, the Dog Star, is ~[the most brightest star]~(A) in the sky with ~[an absolute]~(B) 'magnitude about ~[twenty-three times]~(C) ~[that]~(D) of the Sun.

reason.en: This is the error. The phrase 'the most brightest' is a double superlative. 'Brightest' is already the superlative form of 'bright', so 'most' is redundant and grammatically incorrect. It should be 'the brightest'.
reason.id: Ini adalah bagian yang salah. Frasa 'the most brightest' adalah bentuk superlatif ganda. 'Brightest' sudah merupakan bentuk superlatif dari 'bright', sehingga penggunaan 'most' menjadi berlebihan dan tidak tepat secara tata bahasa. Seharusnya cukup 'the brightest'.

reason.en: This phrase is correct. 'An absolute magnitude' is a standard astronomical term used to describe the intrinsic brightness of a star, and the article 'an' is correctly used before the vowel sound of 'absolute'.
reason.id: Frasa ini sudah benar. 'An absolute magnitude' adalah istilah astronomi standar yang digunakan untuk mendeskripsikan kecerahan intrinsik sebuah bintang, dan artikel 'an' digunakan dengan tepat sebelum bunyi vokal 'absolute'.

reason.en: This phrase is correct. 'Twenty-three times' is a standard way to express a numerical multiplier in English, and it is used correctly here to compare the magnitude of Sirius to the Sun.
reason.id: Frasa ini sudah benar. 'Twenty-three times' adalah cara standar untuk menyatakan pengali numerik dalam bahasa Inggris, dan digunakan dengan tepat di sini untuk membandingkan magnitudo Sirius dengan Matahari.

reason.en: This word is correct. 'That' is used here as a demonstrative pronoun to replace the noun 'magnitude' to avoid repetition, which is a correct and concise grammatical structure.
reason.id: Kata ini sudah benar. 'That' digunakan di sini sebagai kata ganti penunjuk untuk menggantikan kata benda 'magnitude' guna menghindari pengulangan, yang merupakan struktur tata bahasa yang tepat dan ringkas.

questions.tested_skill: Structure & Written Expression - Skill 27: Comparatives and Superlatives

questions.question: Killer whales ~[tend]~(A) ~[to wander]~(B) in family clusters that hunt, play, and ~[resting]~(C) ~[together]~(D).

reason.en: The verb 'tend' is correctly used as the main verb of the sentence, agreeing with the plural subject 'Killer whales'.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'tend' digunakan dengan benar sebagai kata kerja utama kalimat, menyesuaikan dengan subjek jamak 'Killer whales'.

reason.en: The infinitive 'to wander' is correctly used after the verb 'tend'.
reason.id: Infinitive 'to wander' digunakan dengan benar setelah kata kerja 'tend'.

reason.en: This is the error. The sentence uses parallel structure for the verbs following 'that': 'hunt' (base form), 'play' (base form), and 'resting' (present participle). 'Resting' must be changed to 'rest' to maintain parallelism.
reason.id: Ini adalah kesalahannya. Kalimat ini menggunakan struktur paralel untuk kata kerja setelah 'that': 'hunt' (bentuk dasar), 'play' (bentuk dasar), dan 'resting' (present participle). 'Resting' harus diubah menjadi 'rest' untuk menjaga kesejajaran.

reason.en: The adverb 'together' is correctly used to modify the actions of hunting, playing, and resting.
reason.id: Kata keterangan 'together' digunakan dengan benar untuk memodifikasi tindakan berburu, bermain, dan beristirahat.

questions.tested_skill: Structure & Written Expression - Skill 24: Parallel Structure

questions.question: ~[Some of]~(A) the ~[most useful resistor]~(B) ~[material]~(C) are carbon, metals, and ~[metallic]~(D) alloys.

reason.en: The phrase 'Some of' is used correctly here to introduce a portion of a plural noun phrase.
reason.id: Frasa 'Some of' digunakan dengan benar di sini untuk memperkenalkan sebagian dari frasa kata benda jamak.

reason.en: The phrase 'most useful resistor' functions as an adjective phrase modifying the noun. It is grammatically acceptable in this context.
reason.id: Frasa 'most useful resistor' berfungsi sebagai frasa kata sifat yang memodifikasi kata benda. Ini dapat diterima secara tata bahasa dalam konteks ini.

reason.en: This is the error. The verb 'are' indicates that the subject must be plural. Therefore, 'material' should be the plural noun 'materials'.
reason.id: Ini adalah kesalahannya. Kata kerja 'are' menunjukkan bahwa subjek harus berbentuk jamak. Oleh karena itu, 'material' seharusnya menjadi kata benda jamak 'materials'.

reason.en: The word 'metallic' is an adjective correctly modifying the noun 'alloys'.
reason.id: Kata 'metallic' adalah kata sifat yang memodifikasi kata benda 'alloys' dengan benar.

questions.tested_skill: Written Expression - Skill 39: Use the correct singular or plural form

questions.question: ~[The community]~(A) of Bethesda, Maryland, was ~[previous]~(B) ~[known]~(C) ~[as]~(D) Darcy s Store.

reason.en: The phrase 'The community' functions as the subject of the sentence. It is a noun phrase and is grammatically correct.
reason.id: Frasa 'The community' berfungsi sebagai subjek kalimat. Ini adalah frasa kata benda dan secara tata bahasa sudah benar.

reason.en: The word 'previous' is an adjective, but it is used here to modify the verb 'known'. Adverbs are required to modify verbs, so it should be the adverb 'previously'.
reason.id: Kata 'previous' adalah kata sifat, namun di sini digunakan untuk memodifikasi kata kerja 'known'. Kata keterangan (adverb) diperlukan untuk memodifikasi kata kerja, sehingga seharusnya menggunakan kata keterangan 'previously'.

reason.en: The word 'known' is the past participle used in the passive voice construction 'was known as', which is grammatically correct.
reason.id: Kata 'known' adalah past participle yang digunakan dalam konstruksi kalimat pasif 'was known as', yang secara tata bahasa sudah benar.

reason.en: The word 'as' is the correct preposition to follow the verb 'known' when describing a name or identity.
reason.id: Kata 'as' adalah kata depan yang tepat untuk mengikuti kata kerja 'known' saat mendeskripsikan nama atau identitas.

questions.tested_skill: Structure & Written Expression - Skill 46: Adjectives and Adverbs

questions.question: Alloys of gold and copper ~[have]~(A) been ~[widely]~(B) ~[using]~(C) in ~[various types]~(D) of coins.

reason.en: The auxiliary verb 'have' is correct here because it agrees with the plural subject 'Alloys' and is used to form the present perfect tense.
reason.id: Kata kerja bantu 'have' sudah tepat karena sesuai dengan subjek jamak 'Alloys' dan digunakan untuk membentuk present perfect tense.

reason.en: The adverb 'widely' is correctly used to modify the participle 'used', describing the extent of the action.
reason.id: Kata keterangan 'widely' digunakan dengan tepat untuk memodifikasi partisip 'used', menjelaskan cakupan tindakan tersebut.

reason.en: This is the error. The sentence requires a passive voice construction (be + past participle). Since the subject 'Alloys' receives the action, 'using' should be corrected to 'used'.
reason.id: Ini adalah kesalahannya. Kalimat ini memerlukan konstruksi kalimat pasif (be + past participle). Karena subjek 'Alloys' menerima tindakan, 'using' harus diperbaiki menjadi 'used'.

reason.en: The phrase 'various types' is grammatically correct as it functions as the object of the preposition 'in', followed by the prepositional phrase 'of coins'.
reason.id: Frasa 'various types' secara tata bahasa sudah benar karena berfungsi sebagai objek dari preposisi 'in', diikuti oleh frasa preposisional 'of coins'.

questions.tested_skill: Structure & Written Expression - Skill 37: Passive Verbs

questions.question: J. H. Pratt used ~[group]~(A) therapy ~[early]~(B) in this century ~[when he]~(C) brought tuberculosis patients together to discuss ~[its]~(D) disease.

reason.en: The word 'group' is a noun used here as an attributive noun to modify 'therapy', which is grammatically correct.
reason.id: Kata 'group' adalah kata benda yang digunakan di sini sebagai kata benda atributif untuk memodifikasi 'therapy', yang secara tata bahasa sudah benar.

reason.en: The word 'early' is an adverb of time modifying the prepositional phrase 'in this century', which is grammatically correct.
reason.id: Kata 'early' adalah kata keterangan waktu yang memodifikasi frasa preposisional 'in this century', yang secara tata bahasa sudah benar.

reason.en: The phrase 'when he' acts as a correct adverbial clause connector followed by the subject 'he', which is grammatically correct.
reason.id: Frasa 'when he' bertindak sebagai penghubung klausa adverbial yang benar diikuti oleh subjek 'he', yang secara tata bahasa sudah benar.

reason.en: The possessive pronoun 'its' is incorrect because it refers to the plural noun 'patients'. It should be the plural possessive pronoun 'their' to maintain pronoun-antecedent agreement.
reason.id: Kata ganti kepemilikan 'its' salah karena merujuk pada kata benda jamak 'patients'. Seharusnya menggunakan kata ganti kepemilikan jamak 'their' untuk menjaga kesesuaian antara kata ganti dan antesedennya.

questions.tested_skill: Written Expression - Skills 43-45: Pronoun Agreement

questions.question: The United States has ~[import]~(A) ~[all]~(B) carpet wools in recent years ~[because]~(C) domestic wools are ~[too fine]~(D) and soft for carpets.

reason.en: The word 'import' is incorrect because it is the base form of the verb. Following the auxiliary verb 'has', the past participle form 'imported' is required to form the present perfect tense.
reason.id: Kata 'import' salah karena merupakan bentuk dasar kata kerja. Setelah kata kerja bantu 'has', diperlukan bentuk past participle 'imported' untuk membentuk present perfect tense.

reason.en: The word 'all' is used correctly here as a determiner modifying the noun phrase 'carpet wools', indicating the entirety of the supply.
reason.id: Kata 'all' digunakan dengan benar di sini sebagai penentu yang memodifikasi frasa kata benda 'carpet wools', menunjukkan keseluruhan pasokan.

reason.en: The word 'because' is a correct subordinating conjunction used to introduce the reason clause explaining why the imports are necessary.
reason.id: Kata 'because' adalah kata hubung subordinatif yang tepat untuk memperkenalkan klausa alasan yang menjelaskan mengapa impor tersebut diperlukan.

reason.en: The phrase 'too fine' is grammatically correct as it functions as an adjective phrase describing the domestic wools, indicating a degree that exceeds what is suitable for carpets.
reason.id: Frasa 'too fine' secara tata bahasa benar karena berfungsi sebagai frasa kata sifat yang mendeskripsikan wol domestik, menunjukkan tingkat yang melebihi apa yang cocok untuk karpet.

questions.tested_skill: Structure & Written Expression - Skill 32: Use of the Verb (Form of the Verb)

questions.question: Irving Berlin ~[wrote]~(A) "Oh How I Hate to Get Up in the Morning" ~[while serving]~(B) in ~[a]~(C) U.S. Army ~[during]~(D) World War I.

reason.en: [please evaluate-manual] The verb 'wrote' is the simple past tense, which is grammatically correct for a completed action in the past (during World War I). However, as this is marked as the incorrect option, it implies a potential error in the source key or a specific stylistic preference for a different verb form, though 'wrote' is standard.
reason.id: [please evaluate-manual] Kata kerja 'wrote' adalah bentuk lampau (simple past tense), yang secara tata bahasa sudah benar untuk tindakan yang selesai di masa lalu (selama Perang Dunia I). Namun, karena ini ditandai sebagai opsi yang salah, ini mengindikasikan adanya kemungkinan kesalahan pada kunci jawaban sumber atau preferensi gaya bahasa tertentu, meskipun 'wrote' adalah bentuk yang standar.

reason.en: The phrase 'while serving' is a correct reduced adverbial clause, meaning 'while he was serving'. It is grammatically sound.
reason.id: Frasa 'while serving' adalah klausa adverbial yang disingkat (reduced adverbial clause) yang benar, yang berarti 'while he was serving'. Ini secara tata bahasa sudah tepat.

reason.en: The article 'a' is correctly used before the singular countable noun phrase 'U.S. Army'.
reason.id: Artikel 'a' digunakan dengan benar sebelum frasa kata benda tunggal yang dapat dihitung 'U.S. Army'.

reason.id: Preposisi 'during' digunakan dengan benar untuk menunjukkan periode waktu (Perang Dunia I).

questions.tested_skill: WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 33: Use of the Verb (Tense)

questions.question: Banks ~[are rushing]~(A) to merge because consolidations ~[enable them]~(B) to slash ~[theirs]~(C) costs and ~[expand]~(D).

reason.en: The verb 'are rushing' is correct as it agrees with the plural subject 'Banks' in the present continuous tense.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'are rushing' sudah tepat karena sesuai dengan subjek jamak 'Banks' dalam bentuk present continuous tense.

reason.en: The phrase 'enable them' is correct; 'enable' is the verb following the plural subject 'consolidations', and 'them' is the correct object pronoun referring back to 'Banks'.
reason.id: Frasa 'enable them' sudah tepat; 'enable' adalah kata kerja yang mengikuti subjek jamak 'consolidations', dan 'them' adalah kata ganti objek yang benar untuk merujuk kembali ke 'Banks'.

reason.en: The word 'theirs' is incorrect. It is a possessive pronoun that stands alone. In this context, a possessive adjective ('their') is required to modify the noun 'costs'.
reason.id: Kata 'theirs' salah. Itu adalah kata ganti kepunyaan (possessive pronoun) yang berdiri sendiri. Dalam konteks ini, diperlukan kata sifat kepunyaan (possessive adjective) yaitu 'their' untuk menerangkan kata benda 'costs'.

reason.en: The verb 'expand' is correct because it is parallel to the verb 'slash' following the infinitive structure 'to slash... and [to] expand'.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'expand' sudah tepat karena sejajar (paralel) dengan kata kerja 'slash' setelah struktur infinitif 'to slash... and [to] expand'.

questions.tested_skill: Written Expression - Skill 44: Possessive Adjectives vs. Possessive Pronouns

questions.question: ~[That]~(A) water has a very high specific heat ~[means]~(B) that without a large temperature change water can ~[add or lose]~(C) a large ~[number]~(D) of heat.

reason.en: The word 'That' correctly introduces the noun clause 'That water has a very high specific heat', which functions as the subject of the sentence.
reason.id: Kata 'That' dengan tepat mengawali klausa kata benda 'That water has a very high specific heat', yang berfungsi sebagai subjek kalimat.

reason.en: The verb 'means' is correctly conjugated in the singular form to agree with the singular noun clause subject.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'means' dikonjugasikan dengan benar dalam bentuk tunggal agar sesuai dengan subjek klausa kata benda yang bersifat tunggal.

reason.en: The phrase 'add or lose' is grammatically correct as it follows the modal 'can' and maintains parallel structure.
reason.id: Frasa 'add or lose' secara tata bahasa benar karena mengikuti modal 'can' dan mempertahankan struktur paralel.

reason.en: The word 'number' is incorrect because it is used with the uncountable noun 'heat'. 'Number' is for countable nouns; 'amount' should be used for uncountable nouns.
reason.id: Kata 'number' salah karena digunakan bersama kata benda yang tidak dapat dihitung (uncountable) yaitu 'heat'. 'Number' digunakan untuk kata benda yang dapat dihitung; seharusnya menggunakan 'amount' untuk kata benda yang tidak dapat dihitung.

questions.tested_skill: Written Expression - Skill 39: Use the correct singular or plural form (Countable vs. Uncountable Nouns)

questions.question: Benny Goodman was ~[equally]~(A) talented as both a jazz ~[performer]~(B) ~[as well as a]~(C) ~[classical musician]~(D).

reason.id: Kata 'equally' digunakan dengan benar sebagai kata keterangan yang memodifikasi kata sifat 'talented' untuk menjelaskan tingkat keahliannya.

reason.id: Kata 'performer' adalah kata benda yang digunakan dengan benar di bagian pertama struktur paralel setelah kata 'as'.

reason.en: This is the error. The sentence uses the correlative conjunction 'as... as'. Therefore, 'as well as a' should be replaced with 'as' to maintain parallel structure.

reason.en: The phrase 'classical musician' is a noun phrase that correctly completes the second part of the comparison.
reason.id: Frasa 'classical musician' adalah frasa kata benda yang melengkapi bagian kedua dari perbandingan tersebut dengan benar.

questions.tested_skill: Written Expression - Skill 24: Parallel Structure

questions.question: The state seal ~[still used]~(A) in Massachusetts ~[designed by]~(B) Paul Revere, ~[who]~(C) also designed ~[the first]~(D) Continental currency.
